todaysEvents.summary: Oil tumbles -5%: Rubio says 'give talks every chance to succeed'; White House calls Iran MOU claim 'complete fabrication' (May 27)
todaysEvents.delta: Neutral: Two perverse signals May 27 - Rubio says US will give Iran talks 'every chance to succeed' (CNBC) pushing oil -5% on deal hopes, but White House immediately calls Iran state TV MOU claim a 'complete fabrication' - contradicting 'largely negotiated' narrative from May 23. US forces launched fresh strikes in southern Iran (Pentagon: defensive); Tehran vowed retaliation (Tasnim). Brent $94.29 confirmed (CNBC May 27 close -5%); WTI $88.68 confirmed (-5%). Dow hit new record on oil decline despite uncertainty.

todaysEvents.summary: Fresh US strikes in Iran; IRGC targets US air base; US intercepts Iranian drones (May 28)
todaysEvents.delta: Up: US forces launched additional strikes in Iran against a military site threatening troops and Hormuz shipping (CNBC May 28; US official). Iran's Revolutionary Guard said it targeted a US air base at ~4:50am local time (Tasnim news agency). Several Iranian drones intercepted and downed. Brent +2% intraday to $96.28; WTI +2% to $90.75. Trump: 'no nation will control the strait' -international waters.

todaysEvents.summary: Ceasefire extension deal reported; S&P 500 +0.3% to fresh all-time high; Apr PCE 3.8% YoY in-line (May 28)
todaysEvents.delta: Down: Negotiators reached deal to extend Iran ceasefire per report (CNBC May 28) - but traders wary. S&P 500 +0.3% to fresh all-time high 7,542; Dow hit new record May 27 on oil decline. April PCE 3.8% annual in-line (Commerce Dept). ADNOC CEO: 4 months to 80% Hormuz flows even if conflict ends; full normalisation Q1-Q2 2027. Tail 28% (from 23% D85).

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Trump declared deal 'largely negotiated' May 23 (CNBC, CNN, NPR) but White House called MOU claim 'complete fabrication' May 27. Fresh US strikes in Iran + IRGC retaliation (May 28) reopen military risk. Ceasefire extension reportedly close (May 28). Phase 1 framework: Hormuz reopens, Iran clears mines, Iran oil sales resume, US lifts blockade + sanctions waivers; Phase 2 nuclear talks 30-60 days. If signature confirmed: Brent retreats toward $80-85/bbl; P&I restoration enables commercial traffic; Ras Laffan restart timeline ~late Aug 2026. Reduced from D85 30% - White House denial + renewed strikes reduce deal probability. ADNOC CEO: 4 months to 80% flows even if conflict ends.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Deal signed but implementation crawls: Phase 1 Hormuz partial reopening while nuclear sequencing dispute delays Phase 2; Khamenei uranium directive creates Phase 2 red line; mine-clearing ~6 months delays full commercial recovery; P&I clubs restore conditionally; oil stabilises $85-105/bbl (lower on Rubio deal optimism). TTF €42-50/MWh; JKM $15-20/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG 165-200bp. Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026 even on deal. Dominant scenario (47%) - partial normalisation, not resolution.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Deal collapses before signature: White House 'fabrication' denial + fresh US strikes + IRGC retaliation (May 27-28) reopen military escalation. Khamenei uranium directive vs US nuclear-first demand unresolved. Iran's Fars Strait control dispute. Brent spikes $125-165/bbl; Kharg Island seizure or GCC production targeting; Malaysia July-August supply crisis; iTraxx spikes to 230-260bp. Tail (28%) - increased from D85 23% on renewed US-Iran military exchanges; Khamenei directive + Strait sovereignty + White House denial are collapse triggers.
